name: Ted Fio Rito
description: American composer, orchestra leader, and keyboardist (1900–1971)
abstract: Theodore Salvatore Fiorito (December 20, 1900 – July 22, 1971), known professionally as Ted Fio Rito, was an American composer, orchestra leader, and keyboardist, on both the piano and the Hammond organ, who was popular on American national radio broadcasts in the 1920s and 1930s. His name is sometimes written as Ted Fiorito or Ted FioRi…

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: So when I got up to play for Ted Fio Rito, he offered me a job right there... I was there for three months in that place... Ted Fio Rito’s Big Band
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: my first band that I played with was Ted FioRito... He offered me a job right there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: I'm not sure if it was Ted Fiorito's band, but I know it was one of those bands of that sort that she sang with down the Loop.

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: I had played Charlie Barnet and Gene Krupa, and Ted Fiorito, and a few other bands, territory bands.
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy DeFranco
about_quotes.evidence: Scat Davis, Krupa, Charlie Barnet, Ted Fio Rito, and Boyd Raeburn.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy DeFranco interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 13, 1996
